# Glossary

## Algorithmic Video Transmission / AVT

The working domain name for this research system. AVT studies how short-form videos move through platform recommendation systems by producing observable viewer behaviour.

## Factor

The creative variable being changed. Examples: first frame, hook type, text density, CTA, proof asset, length.

## Level

A version of a factor. Example: the factor `first frame` may have levels `face`, `before/after`, `result-first`, `data screenshot`.

## Response variable

The measured outcome. Examples: chose-to-view, 3-second hold, completion, saves, sends, follows, search traffic.

## Mechanism

The behavioural pathway being tested. Example: first-frame salience, cognitive-load reduction, identity transmission.

## Hypothesis

A testable prediction linking a factor to a response variable through a mechanism.

## Scientific-style name

A stable Latin-style operational label. Example: `*Salientia primafacialis* var. h091-videos-show-payoff`.

## Method code

A named testing methodology. Example: `M-SVT` for single-variable variant test.

## Experiment code

A unique identifier for a real experiment. Example:

```text
EXP-20260421-YTS-SHRT-ATT.PRIM-M-MPT-FRAME5
```
